title: 250 years of American independence: A disenchanted anniversary
subhead: 'America 250' (13/13). Trump's two terms in office are undermining national cohesion. The ideal of equality enshrined in the founding documents is a distant prospect, the system of checks and balances has cracked and any reform seems impossible. Americans' belief in their shared history is eroding. Even the nature of the system of government is a matter of debate. But historians are trying to put things into perspective.

'Clipification': How short-form videos are reshaping the media landscape
subhead: While not a new concept, clips have become, over just a few years, the dominant form of expression on the internet, extending far beyond advertising. The cultural and cognitive effects of this format are now a subject of debate.

title: Pope urges Europe to protect migrants in visit to Lampedusa island
subhead: Pope Leo XIV visited the island, a key destination for those risking the perilous Mediterranean crossing from Africa to Europe, and delivered a speech calling on the US and EU to change their migration strategies.

title: Paul Seixas, the 19-year-old phenom carrying France's Tour hopes
subhead: For his first appearance at the Tour de France, which begins Saturday in Barcelona, the 19-year-old French cyclist is already seen as one of the top rivals to four-time winner Tadej Pogacar.

title: 'With Trump, the Founding Fathers' belief in the exemplary nature of US presidents has been lost'
subhead: The US president has broken with the moral constraints that weighed on his predecessors. As the US prepares to celebrate 250 years of independence, pessimism has prevailed in the country, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

'Which side were you on during Brexit? Every candidate in the French presidential election should answer this question'
subhead: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
subhead: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.
